sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, Matthew Rogers, presents for an acne follow-up appointment.
He reports that his acne is doing better since the last visit on 3/3/21.
He has been using the prescribed medications, including clindamycin lotion, tretinoin cream, 4-5% benzoyl peroxide wash for the face, and 5-10% benzoyl peroxide wash for the chest and back.
He has not experienced any redness, peeling, dryness, or irritation with the use of these medications.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications:
Clindamycin lotion
Tretinoin cream 4-5%
Benzoyl peroxide wash 4-5% for the face
Benzoyl peroxide wash 5-10% for the chest and back
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: Scattered inflammatory papules and pustules across the upper back and posterior shoulders, less than 5 inflammatory papules on the forehead, cheeks, and chin.
Acne vulgaris is improved and well-controlled on the face, but there is a mild flare on the back.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's acne vulgaris is improved and well-controlled on the face, but there is a mild flare on the back.
Problem list: Acne vulgaris; Acne flare on the back
Plan:
I recommended continuing the current treatment plan for the face, which includes clindamycin lotion, tretinoin cream 4-5%, and 4-5% benzoyl peroxide wash.
I also initiated treatment with PanOxyl 10% foaming wash for the back, to be used daily in the shower.
I prescribed PanOxyl 10% foaming wash to help control the mild flare on the back.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in one year for a follow-up appointment to review his acne treatment plan and ensure that he has enough medication.
